A Documentary Guide

The Reykjavík Summit

Reagan and Gorbachev at Höfði House · 11–12 October 1986

Participation Register

Who is present in the record, month by month, from the first preparation paper in March 1986 to the end of the administration — drawn from the Office of the Historian’s person annotations on every document. Each row is a participant; each cell is a month, shaded by how many documents that person appears in. Numbered editorial notes mark episodes legible in the record itself. Select a row to filter the timeline and the explorer.

United States
Soviet Union
Other / unattributed
Curated delegation roster

Cell shading: documents per month (1 · 2–3 · 4–6 · 7+). Sides are inferred from the volumes’ lists of persons. Undated editorial notes count toward totals but not the monthly grid.

Register

Editorial notes

    Höfði House — The Two Days

    Press play to follow the delegations through the six documented meetings of 11–12 October 1986. Attendance is exactly as printed in each memorandum’s list of participants; meeting times are taken from the memcon datelines. Figures return to their delegations between meetings. Select a figure to filter the other views.

    Hour-by-Hour Timeline

    Anchored to the Iceland Chronology in Foreign Relations of the United States, 1981–1988, Volume VI, Document 1 — a Memorandum for the Record dated 18 October 1986 that reconstructs the four days in Iceland. Documents released in Volumes V and VI are folded in as marker events; times of day are parsed from the chronology text. Photographs from the White House Photographic Collection (Ronald Reagan Presidential Library) are placed alongside the moments they depict on 11–12 October 1986.

    FRUS Document Explorer

    A searchable, filterable table of every document in the corpus. FRUS records are compiled and edited by the Office of the Historian; declassified releases are drawn from the Department of State’s Virtual Reading Room. Each record links to its canonical publisher URL.

    Date Source Title Session Topics
    Select a document to open its record here.

    Declassified PDFs from FOIA 1986-04261

    The complete declassified release for FOIA case — . Every document is served locally from this site; each entry also links back to its canonical PDF in the U.S. Department of State’s Virtual Reading Room. Search by document number, then open the PDF in a new tab.
